channel.title: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
channel.description: Join GMBN for some insider insights with some of biggest names in downhill, cross country and enduro for an in-depth chat drenched with anecdotes, insights and opinions from the world of mountain biking.

image.title: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

channel.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 571: Have eBikes Killed The Enduro Bike? | Dirt Shed Show 571
item.itunes:title: Have eBikes Killed The Enduro Bike? | Dirt Shed Show 571
item.description:
This week on the Dirt Shed Show, James Pickering and Owen Coutts dive into the controversial question of whether enduro bikes have a future or if they are being squeezed out by ultra-capable trail bikes and lightweight e-MTBs. We also take a look at the bold new Mondraker XC kit, share some incredible bike builds in the Bike Vault, and announce our latest competition winners.

⏱ Timestamps ⏱
0:00 - The Future of Enduro
01:28 - E-Bikes vs. Enduro
07:58 - Giveaway Winners
08:19 - Hacks & Bodges
08:52 - Things We Like
11:08 - Double Backflips & Moguls
12:28 - Caption Contest
14:36 - The Bike Vault
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 169: What’s The Scariest Race In Mountain Biking? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: What’s The Scariest Race In Mountain Biking? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Welcome back to the Dirt Shed Show! This week, we dive into the ultimate debate: which is truly scarier, the high-stakes 80ft gaps of Red Bull Hardline or the 1,300km brutal isolation of the Atlas Mountain Race? We also round up the latest results from the Snowbike World Champs.

⏱ Timestamps ⏱
00:00 - What Is The Scariest MTB Event?
01:28 - Atlas Mountain Race vs. Red Bull Hardline
05:53 - News
08:24 - Giveaway!
08:51 - Hacks & Bodges: Cable Sorcery & Blue Tack Fixes
12:24 - Things We Liked This Week
14:25 - Caption Contest
15:51 - The Bike Vault
20:05 - Coming Up

The Global Mountain Bike Network (GMBN) is the world’s largest and fastest-growing online mountain bike channel and community – and your destination for the best mountain bike content in the world.

GMBN is dedicated to inspiring and unlocking your riding potential. Our videos cater to fans of every mountain bike discipline: from mile-munching cross-country (XC) riders, through adrenaline-fuelled dirt jumpers, gravity-hungry downhillers or aspiring enduro racers, and beyond. 

Our ex-pro presenting team are on hand to entertain and inspire you while also providing a uniquely qualified insight into the world of mountain biking. Every week we deliver original daily videos that include:

item.title: 168: What Makes A Good Mountain Biker? | Dirt Shed Show 569
item.itunes:title: What Makes A Good Mountain Biker? | Dirt Shed Show 569
item.description: Rich and James dive deep into the ultimate debate: what exactly defines a perfect all-round mountain bike rider?  We break down whether you need to dominate multiple disciplines to be considered the best. Plus, we’ve got a legendary "Hacks and Bodges" segment and a look at some stunning custom builds in the Bike Vault!
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 167: Should There Be An Age Limit For Mountain Bike Events? | Dirt Shed Show 568
item.itunes:title: Should There Be An Age Limit For Mountain Bike Events? | Dirt Shed Show 568
item.description:
This week on the Dirt Shed Show, James Pickering and Fergus Ryan ask if it’s time for mountain bike events to introduce tighter age restrictions after watching 11-year-old Wes Sends backflip a 60-foot double. We also dive into the tech of the brand-new Orbea Rallon RS, featuring the Fox Live Valve Neo wireless system. Plus, we’ve got your weekly dose of Hacks and Bodges and a 'Super Nice' session in the Bike Vault!

Enter the FSA competition giveaway 👉 https://gmbn.eu/FSAHandlebarComp2026Q1 

This competition is only open to those residing in: EU, UK, NZ, US, Canada and Alaska. Entries close 10:00GMT Friday 30th January 2026, and winners will be announced in a Dirt Shed Show from Tuesday 3rd February 2026.

item.title: 166: What To Look Forward To In 2026! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: What To Look Forward To In 2026! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Welcome to the first Dirt Shed Show of 2026! This week, Rich Payne and James Pickering dive into the massive team changes shaking up the pro circuit and the controversial rise of 32-inch wheels in World Cup XC. Plus, we look at the $100,000 prize purses up for grabs this season and showcase some of the gnarliest trail-side fixes in Hacks & Bodges.

item.title: 165: Our Favorite Moments Of 2025 | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Our Favorite Moments Of 2025 | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Happy New Year from everyone at GMBN HQ! Join Rich, Fergus, and James as they look back at a hectic year of riding, from summiting Europe’s highest peaks to surviving the adrenaline-fueled chaos of urban downhill. We’re also teasing our massive plans for 2026, including some brutal endurance races and a return to the downhill circuit.

⏱ Timestamps ⏱
00:00 - Happy New Year from the Dirt Shed!
01:00 - Summiting Europe’s Highest Peak
02:10 - Riding Death Slabs with Remy Metallier
02:54 - XC Bike vs. World Cup Downhill Track
04:25 - Wholesome Bikepacking with Dad
05:25 - Surviving Genoa Urban Downhill
07:11 - Riding the Peak District
08:18 - Underground Mountain Biking in Caves
09:25 - Racing Ardrock Enduro
10:33 - Big Teasers for 2026!

item.title: 164: Festive Fails, Questionable Gifts, And The Big Christmas Dinner Debate! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Festive Fails, Questionable Gifts, And The Big Christmas Dinner Debate! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Merry Christmas from the Dirt Shed! This week, Ferg and James dive into the ultimate festive dilemma: will a 6,000-calorie Christmas dinner actually ruin your riding fitness for next year? We also look back at our biggest fails and crashes from our first six months on the channel,

item.title: 162: Do Dream Bikes Still Exist? | Dirt Shed Show Ep. 566
item.itunes:title: Do Dream Bikes Still Exist? | Dirt Shed Show Ep. 566
item.description:
Martyn Ashton returns to the shed to discuss whether "Dream Bikes" are still relevant in an era where entry-level mountain bikes are better than ever. Martyn also announces the official launch of Rideable Now, his new platform dedicated to adaptive mountain biking, featuring an incredible £10,000 Hope HB916 giveaway. Plus, we check out your snowy bike shots in the Vault and crown a life-saving winner of Hacks & Bodges!

Check out RideableNow 👉 https://gmbn.eu/RideableNow to help

item.title: 161: Is Wearing Protection Putting You In Danger? | Dirt Shed Show 562
item.itunes:title: Is Wearing Protection Putting You In Danger? | Dirt Shed Show 562
item.description: Rich Payne and Fergus Ryan are back in the Dirt Shed, and they've got a bone to pick with Owen Coutts from GMBN Tech! We dive deep into Owen's controversial take that riders should slow down rather than rely on body armour and full-face helmets. Plus, we've got the latest mountain bike news, a crash story, an Enduro legend retiring, and a fresh round of Hacks & Bodges.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 160: Are Mountain Bikers Too Vain? | Dirt Shed Show 561
item.itunes:title: Are Mountain Bikers Too Vain? | Dirt Shed Show 561
item.description: Welcome back to The Dirt Shed Show! This week, Ferg and Adam dive into a hot topic: are mountain bikers letting vanity get in the way of performance and safety? We discuss everything from rear mudguards and white riding shoes to the vital importance of wearing protection like back protectors. Plus, we cover the latest MTB news, a Giveaway, and all the usuals!
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 159: The Side Of Mountain Biking Nobody Talks About | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: The Side Of Mountain Biking Nobody Talks About | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the Dirt Shed Show, we dive into the hidden side of MTB injuries, recovery, and the inspiring growth of adaptive riding. Martyn shares his personal injury recovery journey and the excitement around his new project, Rideable Now, as we discuss Adolf Silva's incredible progress. Plus, catch up on the news, Hacks & Bodges, and the wildest videos we liked this week!

item.title: 158: Is The Bike Industry Trying To Cancel 29” Wheels? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Is The Bike Industry Trying To Cancel 29” Wheels? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Are we ready to talk about wheel size again?! 🤯 The Dirt Shed Show is back and this week we're tackling the elephant in the room: 32-inch wheels. Neil and James discuss whether these massive wheels will ever move beyond niche XC use, plus we cover big news, bike hacks, and the best riding edits of the week!

item.itunes:title: Is Marketing Ruining Mountain Biking? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Does Mountain biking have too many labels? This week on the Dirt Shed Show, Ferg and James discuss how Mountain biking should not be split into so many sub-categories. We have some very positive news from YT and Nukeproof, and much more of the usuals!

item.title: 156: 99% Of People Buy The Wrong Bike, Have You? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: 99% Of People Buy The Wrong Bike, Have You? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the Dirt Shed Show, Rich Payne and Fergus Ryan dive into how you might have bought yourself the wrong bike. Did you buy the bike that would be perfect for shredding trails in the Alps or a big bike park holiday, where you might have been better off with a more practical bike, like a trail bike for everyday riding?

item.title: 155: Is The UCI Going To Ruin Mountain Biking? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Is The UCI Going To Ruin Mountain Biking? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
In this week’s GMBN Dirt Shed Show, James Pickering is joined by Fergus Ryan to ask a big question: are we living in the golden age of mountain biking, and is it about to end? From UCI rules, F1-style elitism and shrinking finals, to privateers being squeezed out. Is downhill and XC racing becoming too exclusive, and if so, what does that mean for us trail riders? Plus we’ve got Hacks & Bodges, Bike Vault, the caption contest, and all your usual Dirt Shed Show laughs!

item.itunes:title: When Does A Bike Go Out Of Date? | Dirt Shed Show 555
item.description:
In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Rich Payne and James Pickering are asking the big question: Do mountain bikes go out of date? They debate whether modern innovations are necessary by looking at highly capable Enduro bikes from 2017-2018, and discuss the evolution of bike standards. Plus we’ve got Hacks & Bodges, the caption contest, and Bike Vault!

item.itunes:title: Has Red Bull Rampage Gone Too Far? | Dirt Shed Show 554
item.description:
In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Rich Payne is joined by Fergus Ryan, they dive into the 19th edition of Red Bull Rampage, which saw incredible riding, but the cost was huge. They dive into the aftermath, discussing whether the event's evolution from raw freeride to slopestyle is pushing the limits of safety.

item.title: 151: Was Mountain Biking Better 10 Years Ago? | The Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Was Mountain Biking Better 10 Years Ago? | The Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Today on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton joins Rich Payne and Fergus Ryan to break down the big question: Was mountain biking better 10 years ago? They dive into the different bikes and equipment, along with the presence of social media, making an impact on different aspects of riding. in the news, we have the release of the new Crank Brothers Mallet DH pedals and also the new Pirreli XC tyres, which are only available for 29’ tyres.

item.title: 147: Why Do Mountain Bikers SECRETLY Love To Suffer? | Dirt Shed Show 546
item.itunes:title: Why Do Mountain Bikers SECRETLY Love To Suffer? | Dirt Shed Show 546
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS546

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Rich Payne is joined by James Pickering to dive into the world of Type 2 Fun. You know, that kind of fun that feels terrible in the moment but amazing afterwards! From gruelling winter training rides to 24-hour challenges, we discuss why mountain bikers keep chasing this strange mix of suffering and satisfaction. Plus, we’ve got all the usual Dirt Shed goodness: MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks & Bodges, Things We Liked, Caption Contest, and of course…the Bike Vault!

item.itunes:title: Is Specializing In One Discipline Killing Your Riding? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS545

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, we’re asking the big question: what makes the ultimate mountain biker? Is it the jack-of-all-trades like Ryan Gilchrist and Jordy Scott at Crankworx, or the master of one like Loic Bruni in downhill? We’ll also be looking at the latest MTB news, announcing our Camelbak winners, checking out your Hacks & Bodges, and of course, diving into the Bike Vault!

item.title: 145: Is Mountain Biking Growing? | GMBN Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Is Mountain Biking Growing? | GMBN Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS554

This week in the Dirt Shed Show, Rich Payne and Blake Samson talk about record-breaking WBD racing numbers, the paywall debate in MTB coverage, and what it all means for riders, fans, and the sport. We’ve also got Leadville 100 news, Downeyville results, and Crankworx Whistler kicking off! Plus, it’s a bittersweet one as we say goodbye to Blake in his final Dirt Shed Show after 9 incredible years at GMBN.

Camelbak Competition Link! 🔗 https://gmbn.eu/GMBNCamelBakComp

item.itunes:title: 100Ft Drops, 1 Million Foot Climbs? Where Is The Limit? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Blake Samson is joined by James Pickering to ask the question, how far is too far? Some of the world's best riders have taken on some massive challenges, from 100+ft backflips to climbing 1 million feet of elevation in 100 days, and the recent Vinny T huge drop attempt!

Plus the latest MTB news, Sickest Thing, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

item.title: 139: Something Special is Happening! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Something Special is Happening! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton and Rich Payne dive into a truly special moment in mountain biking. Jackson Goldstone has just dominated the past three World Cups in a row! Over in cross-country, Puck Pieterse continues to wow, becoming the first-ever rider to win both XCC and XCO in Val di Sole on the same weekend!

item.title: 138: When Should You Walk Away? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: When Should You Walk Away? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
In case you missed it, Ferg has been out on a trip to Canada recently to film with Garmin's senior product manager in Calgary for the launch of the new Garmin Edge MTB (Video on  GMBN Tech now). Whilst he was there, we managed to squeeze another collab in with Propain athlete Remy Metailler who coached Ferg down some crazy Squamish terrain. High consequence, physically demanding with a crazy high skill level, Ferg ticked off many of the routes down Gouranga, but was smart enough to know when to walk away...

Do you know when to call it quits on the bike? Or do you just keep pushing? Does your luck ever run out? Let us know your experiences!

#gmbn #globalmountainbikenetwork #mtb #mountainbiking #dirtshedshow

item.title: 135: Why You Should Constantly Seek Out New Riding Spots! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Why You Should Constantly Seek Out New Riding Spots! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Should we constantly be on the hunt for new places to ride? We all love our classic riding spots, knowing every turn, root, rock, jump and line, but do we limit ourselves by being creatures of habit? If we were to constantly ride in the same place, would we become less adaptable to different surfaces, levels of grip and onsighting new tracks? After a trip to Poland, a location he's never visited, Rich is chatting through the theories with Fergus.

item.title: 130: How Riding Bikes Can Prevent An Early Death! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: How Riding Bikes Can Prevent An Early Death! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to ask the question, how does age affect mountain biking? We know MTB doesn’t get easier with age, but what do the experts think? We chat to Professor Leigh Breen, a world-leading expert in skeletal muscle physiology, to dig into why mountain biking starts to feel tougher as we get older.

Plus the latest MTB news, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

Watch this episode on YouTube here: https://gcn.eu/DSS528

item.title: 128: Tensions Mount Ahead Of The Season Opener! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Tensions Mount Ahead Of The Season Opener! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton discusses the controversial decision by the UCI Mountain Bike World Series to reduce the podium from five to three riders, despite strong objections from athletes! Martyn also recaps the exciting racing from the Araxa World Cup XC race in Brazil, which saw XC legend Nino Schurter have a mechanical and climb back up to secure a 5th place finish!

item.title: 127: SRAM Go Mechanical... Did Mountain Biking Just Get Simple Again?! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: SRAM Go Mechanical... Did Mountain Biking Just Get Simple Again?! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Blake Samson to chat about SRAM's latest development, Eagle Transmission now has gear cables! We chat about how this new bike tech is said to offer durability, reliability, and affordability without sacrificing performance.

item.title: 126: Our Trail Building Dreams Are Under Threat! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Our Trail Building Dreams Are Under Threat! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Blake Samson to dive into the highs and lows of trail building in the MTB world. From exciting new developments like Greg Callaghan’s ‘Greg’s Granite’ in Ireland and Tomomi Nishikubo’s signature build in Japan, to a surprising threat facing riding spots - Pickleball! Could the rise of this fast-growing sport mean the end of even more skateparks, pump tracks, and bike parks? 🤷‍♂️

item.title: 125: Is Modern Mountain Biking Way Too Difficult? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Is Modern Mountain Biking Way Too Difficult? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Owen Coutts to react to what might just be the most wild event in action sports, Trey Jones’ Florida Swampfest! Plus the incredible endurance event the Cape Epic race in South Africa. Martyn breaks down how modern XC mountain biking demands a new level of skill compared to back in the day!

Plus MTB News from Tom, Sickest Thing from Toff as well as Hacks & Bodges and of course the Bike Vault!

item.title: 124: Why Is EVERYONE SO Divided? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Why Is EVERYONE SO Divided? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS522

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Blake Samson to dive into the great debates that divide us mountain bikers! From the controversial "acoustic MTB" label to the battle between Shimano vs SRAM, and wireless vs mechanical gear shifting. Plus, we talk about our brand-new partnership with MET & Bluegrass, including the launch of the MET Revo Helmet. Let’s settle some of these MTB arguments once and for all!

item.title: 123: A New Hope For Cheaper Bikes In 2025?! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: A New Hope For Cheaper Bikes In 2025?! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Blake Samson to discuss the price wars hitting the bike industry in 2025. With big brands slashing prices and EMTBs now competing directly with traditional mountain bikes, are we entering a new era of cheaper bikes for everyone? Plus, we celebrate 10 years of GMBN, talk about the return of Nukeproof!

💻 Submit SENDS, FAILS, Bike Vault and more to
👉 https://www.gmbn.com/uploader

Do you think e-bike price cuts will put pressure on traditional MTB prices? Will 2025 be the year of the best bike deals ever? 🤔 Now that Martyn has finished his Trail Dogs script for his up-and-coming feature video, now is your chance to let him know if you love or hate the ‘Trail Dogs’ segment in the Dirt Shed Show. Would you like to keep it in the show or not?

item.title: 122: Huge Breakthrough In Mountain Biking!? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Huge Breakthrough In Mountain Biking!? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS520

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne. To check out out a revolutionary AI balance bike robot that’s gone viral by blowing minds with its ability to learn and execute wild new skills far faster than humans! They check out Nineyard’s epic and inspirational recent edit, highlighting that pro riders inspire and influence the public by pushing the boundaries of skill and art by embracing failure as part of their progression. Does that mean that one day the average rider will look up to robots

item.itunes:title: Adapt Or Die! World Cup MTB Is Changing & So Are The Teams | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to talk through all the recent changes to off-road bike racing! Apart from Red Bull Hardline, the 2025 mountain bike season hasn’t even started, but the changes are massive. From huge team moves to a new UCI World Series format. We break down the biggest transfers in DH, XC, and Enduro!

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to ask the question, is this shocking tech going to make a comeback? We look at dual-shock rear suspension from the Manitou FS Mk2 prototype, linkage front suspension forks, skin suits, disk covers, and even Truvative HammerSchmidt!

Plus the latest MTB news, Sickest Thing Of The Week, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

item.itunes:title: The Change To World Cups We Won’t Stand For! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on YouTube here: https://gmbn.eu/WM9M2b

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Owen Coutts to react to and question the new UCI Mountain Bike World Cup changes for the 2025 race season. We hear from Bernard Kerr (Pivot Factory Racing team owner) and Theo Erlangsen (Professional Downhill racer). What do you think about the big changes to mountain bike racing as we know it? Share your thoughts below!

Plus the latest MTB news, Sickest Thing Of The Week, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

item.title: 103: Should Brandon Semenuk Have Won Red Bull Rampage? | Dirt Shed Show 502
item.itunes:title: Should Brandon Semenuk Have Won Red Bull Rampage? | Dirt Shed Show 502
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS502

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Owen Coutts to recap the 2024 Red Bull Rampage. The world’s most epic freeride mountain biking event stirred controversy this year. Brendan Fairclough and his team built an epic, challenging line but only placed 11th. Meanwhile, Brandon Semenuk won his 5th Rampage with huge tricks. Fans are upset, feeling the event is shifting from its freeride roots towards slopestyle. Is Rampage losing its identity? Share your thoughts below!

Plus the latest MTB news, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

Find out more about membership tiers and what's involved here: https://gmbn.eu/YouTubeMembership

It’s the 500th show! 🥳 This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton and Neil Donoghue talk about the end of an era in competitive Downhill racing with Greg Minnaar retiring from DH is this the end of the ‘ride hard, party hard’ era of MTB racing?

item.title: 99: Does MTB Racing Actually Sell Bikes? 📈 | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Does MTB Racing Actually Sell Bikes? 📈 | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch this week's show on YouTube here: https://gmbn.eu/DSS497

On this week's Dirt Shed Show Martyn Ashton is joined by Neil Donoghue to ask the question, does bike racing and winning races actually lead to more bike sales? Join us as we question the complex relationship between race wins and brand success.

Plus the latest MTB news, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault!

item.title: 98: 2 Million Subscriber Special! + Exclusive Gee Atherton RIDGELINE V Interview | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: 2 Million Subscriber Special! + Exclusive Gee Atherton RIDGELINE V Interview | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS496

On this week's Dirt Shed Show we're celebrating a huge milestone for the channel... 2 MILLION subscribers! As such we're announcing our YouTube Membership program, something super important to us now that we're an independent company again. If you'd like to join the Trail Crew or become a Trail Boss, you can find out more below. As always, we've got the latest MTB news, Sickest Thing Of The Week, Hacks and Bodges, Caption Contest, and of course the Bike Vault AS WELL as an awesome Interview with Gee Atherton about his new project, RIDGELINE V: RESISTANCE!

item.title: 96: Which Geo Matters? Does It Even Make A Difference? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Which Geo Matters? Does It Even Make A Difference? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS494

On this week's Dirt Shed Show we're discussing the long-standing trends in mountain bike geometry and asking where we're going with all these incremental changes? Would it be bette to just make the giant leap to the endpoint? What does that endpoint even look like? Are geometry numbers on a fact sheet alone enough to put you off a potential purchase? All this, the latest MTB news, Hacks, Bodges, Sickest Thing of the Week and more on this week's show.

item.title: 94: Drop Bar MTB - The Worst New Trend? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Drop Bar MTB - The Worst New Trend? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS492

In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to ask the question, should you rush out to buy some road bike drop bars for your bike? Because, it was only last week that Neil told mountain biking that high-rise bars are the hot new trend! This subject was brought on by the Frankenstein setup that a few riders were using at the Leadville 100! Have gravel bikes really just become mountain bikes with curly handlebars? 🤔

item.title: 93: The Biggest Superstar In MTB? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: The Biggest Superstar In MTB? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS491

In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Neil Donoghue to ask the question, who is the biggest superstar in mountain biking? They discuss Olympic athletes such as Pauline Ferrand-Prévot, Evie Richards, and Tom Pidock. To Trials riders such as Danny Macaskill and Fabio Wibmer, to Dirt Jump/Street/Skatepark riders such as Matt Jones and Sam Pilgrim!

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and Hacks And Bodges, plus some Super Nice bikes locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

item.itunes:title: Why Did They Make The Olympic Track So Boring?! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to discuss the 2024 Olympic Cross County mountain biking, was the XC course too easy at the 2024 Paris Olympics? 🤔 Plus we recap on Crankworx Whistler and react to Kieran Reilly riding skatepark on a Brompton folding bike!

Tom Bower reports the MTB News, we show the best Hacks And Bodges, and share some Nice and Super Nice bikes in the Bike Vault.

item.description: In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Neil Donoghue to discuss whether or not Downhill Mountain Biking should be made into an Olympic sport. Have you ever wondered if bike tech even makes you a faster, or more confident rider? Does being a bike nerd who knows everything about bike tech even matter to you? Some people seem to love bike tech so much and they are more interested with bikes than they are with riding them!

item.title: 89: Will Bikes Become A Subscription-Based Product? | Dirt Shed Show 487
item.itunes:title: Will Bikes Become A Subscription-Based Product? | Dirt Shed Show 487
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS487

In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Rich Payne to ask the question, will the future of mountain bike tech be subscription-based? Martyn’s been obsessed with Tesla lately and thinks the bike industry might start to charge subscriptions. With more and more electronic mtb tech he thinks hardware can be built and later improved with software subscriptions, similar to Tesla’s self-driving capability subscription! 

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and Hacks And Bodges, plus some Super Nice bikes locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

item.title: 81: Bike Accessories That Spank Your Bank! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Bike Accessories That Spank Your Bank! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
Watch the show on our YouTube Channel: https://gmbn.eu/DSS479

This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Owen Coutts AKA 'Owen The Bike Nerd' to talk about mountain bike accessories. Back in the 90's you could simply get a plastic mudguard and a chain device and people would be able to tell you were a 'real rider'! Martyn thinks that in 2024 you need to spend thousands on fancy tech! 😳

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and Hacks And Bodges, plus some Super Nice bikes locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

item.title: 74: Revolt in MTB!! World’s Best Riders Strike at Crankworx | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Revolt in MTB!! World’s Best Riders Strike at Crankworx | Dirt Shed Show
item.description: It’s the Dirt Shed Show and this week Martyn Ashton and Blake Samson are sitting down to discuss the burning issue in mountain biking, the rider strike which affected the Men’s Slopestyle Final at Crankworx Rotorua. The male riders chose not to partake in the competition after travelling to the venue, issuing a list of demands to the organisers who were only able to meet 2 out of the 3 conditions for which the riders would drop in. The sticking point, is over appearance fees, guaranteeing pay for riders who turn up to ride in the competition. We’re reflecting on what this could mean for the rest of mountain biking, and if danger pay for all is feasible with the industry in it’s current condition.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.itunes:title: The Least Talked About Key To Success? (Feat. Jake Atkinson) | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Neil Donoghue and Blake Samson are joined by rising slopestyle rider Jake Atkinson! With Jake's dad reaching out to us, we're focusing on the impact of RAD parents. Do they help with success? Plus, should parents get their kids into the bikelife? 

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and we check out some super nice MTBs locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

#DirtShedShow #GMBN #GlobalMountainBikeNetwork #MTB #MountainBiking

item.title: 70: The Greatest Female Racers Of All Time! | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: The Greatest Female Racers Of All Time! | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on the GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Anna Cipullo to celebrate International Women’s Day! 🚴‍♀️ Join us for a lively debate on the greatest in women’s MTB, as we discuss icons like Tracy Moseley, Rachel Atherton, Isabeau Courdurier, and Veronique Sandler. As well as the greatest winning mountain biker of all time, Anne-Caroline Chausson 🏆🏁

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and we check out some super nice MTBs locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

#DirtShedShow #GMBN

item.title: 69: Are Mountain Bikers Ignorant? (Feat. Hans Rey) | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: Are Mountain Bikers Ignorant? (Feat. Hans Rey) | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
This week on GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Neil Donoghue and Blake Samson are joined by legendary mountain bike pioneer and trials bike superstar Hans Rey to ask the question does mountain biking have an identity crisis? Are mountain bikers arrogant? 

Plus MTB News, Sickest Thing, Hacks And Bodges, Caption Contest, and we check out some super nice MTBs locked away in the Bike Vault!⁠

#DirtShedShow #GMBN #GlobalMountainBikeNetwork #MTB #MountainBiking

item.title: 64: How To Solve The Cost Of Biking Crisis? | Dirt Shed Show
item.itunes:title: How To Solve The Cost Of Biking Crisis? | Dirt Shed Show
item.description:
In this week's GMBN Dirt Shed Show, Martyn Ashton is joined by Blake Samson to discuss their solutions to the 'Cost of biking crisis!'. Will they be able to discover the solution to the high prices of bikes? Are the prices actually too high? In the rest of the show, we look at the results from last week’s Caption Contest, your usual favourites including some super nice MTBs locked away in the Bike Vault and much more!

Watch this episode on YouYube: https://gmbn.eu/DSS462

The Global Mountain Bike Network (GMBN) is the world’s largest and fastest-growing online mountain bike channel and community – and your destination for the best mountain bike content in the world.

GMBN is dedicated to inspiring and unlocking your riding potential. Our videos cater to fans of every mountain bike discipline: from mile-munching cross-country (XC) riders, through adrenaline-fuelled dirt jumpers, gravity-hungry downhillers or aspiring enduro racers, and beyond.

item.title: 62: Is The Bike Industry In Big Trouble? | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: Is The Bike Industry In Big Trouble? | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: We're a little worried about some of the things we're hearing about the bike industry right now. A combination of Covid, external factors and global economies seem to have supply and demand reeling! Martyn sat down with Doddy and Sandy for a hot take on the situation.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 61: Riding To Combat Adversity with Nils From Rider Resilience | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: Riding To Combat Adversity with Nils From Rider Resilience | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: In this episode, Martyn Ashton chats with Nils from Rider Resilience about his story and how mountain biking can help combat adversity. Rider Resilience hopes that having a vast pool of inspirational content can help to foster or regain resilience and positivity in people, that it will serve to appreciate the good things in life and become an ever-growing impetus to once again get out and ride.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 60: Talking Shop With Sandy From The Trailhead Bicycle Co! | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: Talking Shop With Sandy From The Trailhead Bicycle Co! | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: This week we are joined by Sandy Plenty from The Trailhead bicycle company. Sandy has worked in bike shops almost all of his life and has built one of the best bike shops in the UK with a great community focus. We find out how E-Bikes saved his shop, his thoughts on EMTBs Vs MTBs and why he sold one of the most iconic bikes of all time from his personal connection.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 59: From Bullets To Bikes With Ben Deakin | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: From Bullets To Bikes With Ben Deakin | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: The Ben 'Deakinator' Deakin story is one that is very different from the traditional way in which people become pro mountain bikers. From former Royal Marine Commando to MTB Professional Influencer in a matter of just a couple of years and a catchphrase that you just can't forget! #OiOi
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 58: How Extreme is the World Trials Champion? | The Jack Carthy Interview
item.itunes:title: How Extreme is the World Trials Champion? | The Jack Carthy Interview
item.description: This week Martyn is joined by Jack Carthy, Jack is the 9X UCI Elite world champion and multiple UCI World Cup champion of competition bike trials. Jack is one of the most decorated MTBers, but hasn’t had the recognition of riders in other disciplines. What will he do if, or once he reaches his lifetime goal of a 10th world championship title?
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.itunes:title: Managing A Factory Racing Team With Nigel Page | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: This week Martyn is joined by Nigel Page, masters downhill extraordinaire and team manager of the Nukeproof Sram Factory Racing Team (formerly CRC Mavic and team Chain Reaction Cycles). What is it like to manage a team with some superstar EWS athletes and how what is the role of a team manager in their success?

item.itunes:title: How to Have the Most Fun on Your Mountain Bike, With Tracy Moseley | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: Martyn is joined by EWS Champion, Downhill Champion, Downhill World Champion, and Mum Tracy Moseley on the Podcast this week. Listen now as they recount her career, her work for Trek and Bosch, being a mum vs mountain biker, and how to keep riding fun!

item.description: The GMBN Podcast is back with a very special guest, none other than Brook Macdonald! Listen now as he and Martyn discuss his crash and injury at Mont St Anne 2019, his surgery and the recovery steps that followed, and getting back on the bike for 2020's shortened season.

item.title: 51: EWS 2020 Round 3, Finale Ligure | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: EWS 2020 Round 3, Finale Ligure | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: Just like that, the weekends racing in Finale Ligure marks the end of the Enduro World Series 2020 season. Ric, Ruaridh, and Duncan recap all the action, speculate on signings over the off season, and discuss the changes in bike setup over the years

item.title: 50: EWS & EWS-E 2020 Round 2: Pietra Ligure | The GMBN Podcast
item.itunes:title: EWS & EWS-E 2020 Round 2: Pietra Ligure | The GMBN Podcast
item.description: Neil is joined by Ric McLauglin and Duncan Philpott as they recap all the action from the weekends racing of the penultimate round of the 2020 Enduro World Series. Sudden thunderstorms meant a change in conditions for finals, the French riders had a field day, and a couple of crashes meant early baths for Martin Maes and Robin Wallner. Listen now for all the details

item.description: The GMBN Podcast returns along with the first round of the 2020 Enduro World Series and EWS-E from home of the Toblerone - Zermatt, Switzerland. Neil and Ric bring you a recap of the weekend's racing; discussing the challenges of holding an event during a pandemic, the less than ideal conditions and how the riders dealt with them, and talk the significance of the inaugural EWS-E event.

item.itunes:title: What Does It Take To Become An Olympic Mountain Biker? | The GMBN Podcast Ep. 40
item.description: Martyn is joined by Oli Beckingsale in this episode of the GMBN podcast; a Cross Country rider who took part in the 2000, 2004, and 2008 Olympic Mountain Biking.  They discuss what it takes to become an Olympic Athlete, the change in the process and event over the years, and whether British Cycling is biased towards Track cycling.

item.description: The last ten years have seen mountain bike technology advance rapidly. From geometry to tubeless tyres, the bikes we ride are a far cry from that available in 2009. item.title: 5: GMBN presents The Mountain Bike Podcast #Ep.5 XC Nove Mesto Featuring Oli Beckingsale
item.itunes:title: GMBN presents The Mountain Bike Podcast #Ep.5 XC Nove Mesto Featuring Oli Beckingsale
item.description:
Round 2 of the UCI Cross Country World Cup has just taken place in Nové Město, Czech Republic. Neil welcomes back Oli Beckingsale to pick his brains on overtraining, bike choice, the importance of a good start to the season, and the Cape Epic Endurance race.
Two clear battles are emerging in the mens and womens fields; Kate Courtney once again beat Jolanda Neff to the post, whilst in the mens Nino Schurter returns to form to finish second behind jack of all trades Mathieu Van Der Poel. Will we see another clash at the next round in Andorra?
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 3: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #3 EWS Madeira Featuring Tracey Moseley
item.itunes:title: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #3 EWS Madeira Featuring Tracey Moseley
item.description:
This week on the GMBN podcast we're joined by Downhill and Enduro veteran Tracy Moseley. Chatting about her current role at Trek; making the switch from Downhill to Enduro; the growth and success of the Enduro World Series; the rise of the young guns in the EWS, and new Enduro venues for 2019!

So what are you waiting for? Search ‘The Mountain Bike Podcast’ on:

– Spotify
– Google
– iTunes

And get set to sit back, listen and enjoy!
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 2: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #2 The Life Of A World Cup Mechanic
item.itunes:title: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #2 The Life Of A World Cup Mechanic
item.description:
Hi and welcome to the Mountain Bike Podcast presented by GMBN, I am Neil Donoghue and I’m back, ready for round 2!

For this episode we have an ex world cup race mechanic and the new GMBN tech presenter- Henry Quinney. Does spannering for some of the world’s fastest Downhill racers, like Tracey Hannah, prepare him for life at GMBN?

item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast

item.title: 1: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #1 Maribor
item.itunes:title: The Mountain Bike Podcast by GMBN #1 Maribor
item.description:
Welcome to the Global Mountain Bike Network’s (GMBN) all-new Mountain Bike Podcast for a casual chat with the experts of mountain biking about the biggest and best races on the planet, and much more! 

Co-hosted by Neil Donoghue and Steve Jones, this first episode kicks things off an in-depth look at all the action from the first UCI World Cup Downhill race of the year: Maribor, Slovenia.
item.itunes:author: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
item.dc:creator: GMBN Presents The Mountain Bike Podcast
